metadata_details.purpose: Parser for generic logs migrated from samples-main
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of generic logs in appropriate format
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

analysis.semantic_summary: Parser designed to process JRuby application logs with focus on error tracking, HTTP requests, correlation IDs, and structured key-value pairs. Handles multiple timestamp formats, severity levels, and supports both single-line and multi-line log entries with stack traces.

factors: Complex regex patterns for line grouping

optimization_opportunities: Simplify complex regex patterns for better performance
Add structured error handling for malformed logs
Implement caching for repeated pattern matching
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: jruby_application_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Application Activity (2001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:06:50.199230

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local TIMESTAMP_PATTERN = "^(%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%d[T%s]%d%d:%d%d:%d%d%.%d+)"
local IP_PATTERN = "^%d+%.%d+%.%d+%.%d+$"
local CORRELATION_PATTERN = "^[a-z0-9_]+(-[a-z0-9_]+)+$"

-- Cache common string operations
local format = string.format
local match = string.match
local time = os.time

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output with local vars
    local output = {
        class_uid = 2001,
        class_name = "Application Activity", 
        category_uid = 2,
        category_name = "Application Events",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 200101,
        metadata = {
            version = "1.0.0",
            product = {
                name = "JRuby Application",
                vendor_name = "Generic"
            }
        }
    }

    -- Timestamp transformation with validation
    if record.timestamp then
        local ts = match(record.timestamp, TIMESTAMP_PATTERN)
        if ts then
            -- Convert to UNIX milliseconds
            output.time = os.time({
                year = tonumber(ts:sub(1,4)),
                month = tonumber(ts:sub(6,7)),
                day = tonumber(ts:sub(9,10)),
                hour = tonumber(ts:sub(12,13)),
                min = tonumber(ts:sub(15,16)), 
                sec = tonumber(ts:sub(18,19))
            }) * 1000
        end
    end

    -- Severity mapping with validation
    if record.severity then
        local sev = string.upper(record.severity)
        local severity_map = {
            ERROR = 10,
            WARN = 7,
            INFO = 5,
            DEBUG = 3
        }
        output.severity = severity_map[sev] or 5 -- Default to INFO
    end

    -- IP address validation and transformation
    if record.ip and match(record.ip, IP_PATTERN) then
        output.src_ip = record.ip
    end

    -- Correlation ID with format validation
    if record.correlationId and match(record.correlationId, CORRELATION_PATTERN) then
        output.correlation_id = record.correlationId
    end

    -- Message handling with sanitization
    if record.message then
        output.message = string.gsub(record.message, "[^%g%s]", "") -- Remove control chars
    end

    -- Add default timestamp if missing
    if not output.time then
        output.time = time() * 1000
    end

    -- Final validation of required fields
    if not output.class_uid or output.class_uid == 0 then
        return nil, "Invalid class_uid"
    end

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

-- Cached function references for performance
local _transform = transform
transform = safe_transform

Monitor error rates during initial deployment
